articles.title: The Friction Machine
articles.subtitle: Grooves do not generate backspin. Friction does. And friction is governed by four variables the equipment industry rarely mentions.
articles.excerpt: Backspin on a wedge shot is generated by friction at the impact interface, not by grooves. A forensic look at oblique impact mechanics, cover deformation, the 2010 groove rule, and why your ball and your towel matter more than which wedge you bought.

articles.title: Launch Angle, Spin Loft, and the 15 Yards You're Leaving on the Tee
articles.subtitle: The distance you're missing isn't hiding in your swing speed. It's hiding in the gap between your dynamic loft and your angle of attack.
articles.excerpt: Spin loft — the angular gap between dynamic loft and angle of attack — governs driver distance more than any equipment purchase. Why most amateurs hit down on the driver, what the TrackMan data actually shows, and how a few setup changes recover real yards without swinging harder.

articles.title: What Your Putter Actually Does and Does Not Do at Impact
articles.subtitle: A forensic look at the physics of blade, mallet, and zero-torque designs. The math does not care about marketing.
articles.excerpt: A forensic examination of blade, mallet, and zero-torque designs through the lens of oblique impact mechanics. Why face angle dominates direction, what MOI really buys you, and why the dimpled ball puts a ceiling on all of it.
